findings.message: for (const key in process.env) injects ALL build-environment variables into esbuild compile-time defines; combined with prepublishOnly auto-running this script on npm publish, any CI secret present during publish is baked into the publicly-distributed dist/index.js npm binary
process.env.SECRET_KEY is explicitly injected into the published bundle; if SECRET_KEY is a signing or encryption key set in the build environment, it ships publicly in the npm package
All dependencies in the supabase example use caret ranges (^), allowing unexpected minor/patch upgrades; lower risk because this is example code, but reduces reproducibility
Web search example uses model ID 'openai/gpt-5.2'; this model may not exist and could mislead users copying the example verbatim
"relevant PinMe integration skill" — 'relevant' is a vague quantifier (R01); no criteria given for which skill to pick
"Use clean, responsive HTML/CSS" — 'clean' is a vague adjective without measurable criteria
"Keep the design restrained and readable" — 'restrained' is a vague adjective with no actionable definition
"sensible mobile spacing" — 'sensible' is a vague quantifier; no specific spacing values given
"Keep the response short" — 'short' is vague; no token or sentence budget given
"unrelated logs" — 'unrelated' is vague; criteria for what to exclude are not enumerated
"Do not require JavaScript unless interaction is valuable" — 'valuable' gives Claude no decision criterion
Cross-reference to sibling skills uses vague prose ('any relevant PinMe integration skill') instead of explicit [[slug]] links
"simple manual routing" — 'simple' is a vague adjective; no complexity threshold defined
"some fs capabilities" — 'some' is a vague quantifier (R01); does not specify which APIs are available
"if there is a clear need" — 'clear' is a vague qualifier; no decision criterion provided
"only splitting when isolation is truly needed" — 'truly needed' is vague; no isolation criteria defined
Main pinme skill duplicates email, auth, and share content inline without scope notes pointing to the dedicated skills (pinme-email, pinme-auth, pinme-llm, pinme-share)
Email-sending code (handleSendEmail function and API reference) is duplicated between skills/pinme/SKILL.md and skills/pinme-email/SKILL.md, creating two sources of truth for the same API
